Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Stripe for all lock extension charges using stripe_search_charges with query "lock extension" (or similar). Discovers six relevant charges across two loans. For Mercer (LN-2026-00184): ch_scenario_c66_lockext1 at $375.00 (succeeded), ch_scenario_c66_lockext2 at $375.00 (succeeded), and ch_scenario_c66_lockext3 at $500.00 (succeeded), totaling $1,250.00 collected. For Rivera (LN-2026-00619): ch_scenario_cdc_lockext1 at $375.00 (succeeded), ch_scenario_cdc_lockext2_fail at $450.00 (failed), and ch_scenario_cdc_lockext2_ok at $450.00 (succeeded), totaling $825.00 net collected. The failed charge collected zero funds. Note: the agent may also use stripe_list_charges to browse charges broadly, which surfaces additional lock extension charges from older loans (LN-2024-* series) outside the two-month window.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks for lock extension bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria matching "lock extension" in description, or DocNumber containing "LOCK" (or similar). Discovers three bills for Mercer (LN-2026-00184) and zero bills for Rivera (LN-2026-00619). The three Mercer bills are: BILL-2026-0319-001 ($375.00, DocNumber LOCK-EXT-00184, vendor "Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations", empty Line array with no description), BILL-2026-0402-001 ($375.00, DocNumber LOCKEXT-00184-1, vendor "Keystone Mortgage Partners Operating", description "1st lock extension fee for Evan Mercer loan after build delay"), and BILL-2026-0416-002 ($375.00, DocNumber PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER, vendor "Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations", description "Second lock extension fee held pending cost ownership for Mercer loan"). Total QB amount: $1,125.00. Key discovery: BILL-2026-0319-001 has an empty Line array (no description text), making it harder to find via description-based searches. It may only be discoverable via DocNumber search or by fetching individual bills by ID. This bill is a potential duplicate of BILL-2026-0402-001 since both appear to reference the 1st extension at $375.
OE 3: Retrieve individual Mercer QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_get_bill with IDs BILL-2026-0319-001, BILL-2026-0402-001, and BILL-2026-0416-002 to confirm full details including vendor names, amounts, descriptions, and balances. Discovers the vendor name inconsistency: BILL-2026-0402-001 uses vendor name "Keystone Mortgage Partners Operating" while the other two use "Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations", despite all referencing the same vendor ID (V-KESTONE-OPS). Also discovers BILL-2026-0319-001 has balance $375 (unpaid) and BILL-2026-0416-002 has balance $375 (unpaid, described as "held pending cost ownership"), while BILL-2026-0402-001 has balance $0 (paid).
OE 4: Search QuickBooks for Rivera lock extension bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria matching DocNumber "00619" or "Rivera" or "LOCKEXT-00619" (or similar). Discovers zero matching bills, confirming that both successful Rivera Stripe charges ($375.00 and $450.00) have no corresponding QuickBooks entries at all. The books are $825.00 short on the Rivera file.
OE 5: Search for the Mercer loan in the LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "Mercer" (or similar). Discovers loan LN-2026-00184: borrower Evan Mercer, loan amount $340,000, conventional purchase, assigned LO Carlos Rivera, processor Sofia Reyes, lender Homepoint Financial, property 6923 Sharon Rd Charlotte NC 28210, status processing, rate 6.875%, rate lock date 2026-03-01, rate lock expiration 2026-03-23. Notes indicate new construction purchase with build delays causing rate lock extensions.
OE 6: Search for the Rivera loan in the LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "Rivera" (or similar). This search returns multiple Rivera results because Carlos Rivera is an LO on several loans and Marcus Rivera is a borrower on LN-2026-00619. The agent must disambiguate by matching against the Stripe charge descriptions which reference LN-2026-00619. Discovers loan LN-2026-00619: borrower Marcus Rivera, loan amount $217,000, conventional purchase, assigned LO Brian Mitchell, lender Plaza Home Mortgage, property 9866 Albemarle Rd Charlotte NC 28211, status clear_to_close.
OE 7: List staff records using mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only set to false (or similar) to check the status of the LO assigned to the Rivera file. Discovers that Brian Mitchell (assigned LO on LN-2026-00619) is inactive with termination date 2025-04-15. Also discovers Andre Washington (processor) is inactive with termination date 2024-12-15. This explains why the Rivera lock extension charges were never entered in QuickBooks the responsible staff were terminated and no one picked up the billing task. This is a process breakdown that should be flagged to Grace.
OE 8: Search QuickBooks vendors using quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria matching "Keystone" (or similar) to investigate the vendor name inconsistency on the Mercer bills. Discovers that only one vendor exists with ID V-KESTONE-OPS. The display name inconsistency ("Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations" vs "Keystone Mortgage Partners Operating") is a data entry error, not two separate vendors.
OE 9: Search emails using search_emails with query "lock extension" or "Mercer extension" or "extension fee" (or similar) in the INBOX folder, then repeat the search in the SENT folder (search_emails accepts one folder_name at a time). Key results in SENT: email from Carlos Rivera to Evan Mercer regarding rate lock and extension timing on the Mercer new-build file. Also discovers email from Lauren Mitchell (Fairview Homes builder) to Carlos Rivera regarding updated completion date, confirming builder delays and stating the builder will not cover lock extension fees. Also discovers email from Allison Mercer (Mercer Realty Group, unrelated to borrower) to Carlos Rivera regarding closing failure and lock extension cost disputes on a separate file, CC'd Grace.
OE 10: Search emails using search_emails with query "Rivera" or "Rivera charge" or "Rivera lock" (or similar) in the INBOX folder, then repeat in the SENT folder, to find any billing-related communications about the Rivera file. The agent searches for correspondence about the failed charge and fee issues on LN-2026-00619.
OE 11: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "lock extension" or "extension fee" (or similar). Discovers messages from Camille Foster in the rate-watch channel (C005) about lock extension tracking, and messages from Hana Kim in the loan-processing channel (C002) about logging lock extension fees in QuickBooks. Key discovery: Hana posted about logging the first Mercer extension fee ($375 paid) and setting up a pending $375 QB hold for the second extension with ownership TBD. Also discovers Hana posted a pending $375 lock extension placeholder for LN-2026-00619, awaiting Grace confirmation. Note: Hana's placeholder does not specify which extension number it covers. The 1st Rivera Stripe charge was $375 (matching the placeholder), while the 2nd was $450 -- meaning either the placeholder was intended for the 1st extension and the 2nd extension has no QB coverage at all, or it was intended for the 2nd extension with a $75 shortfall. Either way, neither Rivera extension has a finalized QB bill.
OE 12: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Mercer" or "Mercer extension" or "billing" (or similar). Discovers messages in the loan-processing channel (C002) discussing the Mercer builder delay, extension cost ownership dispute, and Carlos Rivera's communications with the borrower and builder about who pays for the extensions.
OE 13: Look up contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Camille" (or similar) to find Camille Foster's email address: camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com. Also look up contacts_search_contacts with query "Hana" to find Hana Kim's email: hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com. Note: searching "Hana" returns multiple contacts (6 results) the agent must identify the correct Hana Kim at Keystone. Also look up contacts_search_contacts with query "Grace" to find Grace Yamamoto's email: grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com.
OE 14: List Slack channels using channels_list with channel_types "public_channel" (or similar) to identify the general channel. Discovers channel C001 named "general".
OE 15: Create a QuickBooks bill for the Rivera 1st lock extension using quickbooks_create_bill with VendorRef value V-KESTONE-OPS (name "Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations"), Line amount $375.00, description referencing Marcus Rivera LN-2026-00619 and Stripe charge ch_scenario_cdc_lockext1, DueDate (e.g. 30 days from transaction date), Balance $375.00, TotalAmt $375.00, DocNumber LOCKEXT-00619-1 (or similar).
OE 16: Create a QuickBooks bill for the Rivera 2nd lock extension (successful retry) using quickbooks_create_bill with VendorRef value V-KESTONE-OPS (name "Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations"), Line amount $450.00, description referencing Marcus Rivera LN-2026-00619, Stripe charge ch_scenario_cdc_lockext2_ok, and noting the prior failed attempt ch_scenario_cdc_lockext2_fail, DueDate (e.g. 30 days from transaction date), Balance $450.00, TotalAmt $450.00, DocNumber LOCKEXT-00619-2 (or similar). The failed charge should NOT have a separate bill since no funds were collected.
OE 17: Create a QuickBooks bill for the Mercer 3rd lock extension using quickbooks_create_bill with VendorRef value V-KESTONE-OPS (name "Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations"), Line amount $500.00, description referencing Evan Mercer LN-2026-00184 and Stripe charge ch_scenario_c66_lockext3, DueDate (e.g. 30 days from transaction date), Balance $500.00, TotalAmt $500.00, DocNumber LOCKEXT-00184-3 (or similar). This bill captures the actual $500 collected in Stripe for the 3rd extension, which had no existing QB bill.
OE 18: Send email using send_email with sender camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com, recipients including camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com and hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com, cc including grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, subject referencing lock extension billing reconciliation (or similar), and body containing the full reconciliation covering: Mercer file findings (three Stripe charges totaling $1,250 vs three QB bills totaling $1,125, the $125 gap on the 3rd extension, the vendor name inconsistency, the potential duplicate 1st extension bill), Rivera file findings (two successful Stripe charges totaling $825 with zero QB bills, the failed charge documented, inactive LO and processor), the three new QB bills created, and recommended next steps including resolving the Mercer $125 gap, correcting the vendor name, and reassigning active staff to the Rivera file.
OE 19: Post a message in the general channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id C001, payload summarizing the lock extension billing audit findings including the Mercer discrepancy ($500 vs $375 gap, vendor name issue), the Rivera missing bills (now created), the failed-and-retried charge, the inactive staff issue, and a reminder for the team to ensure QB bills are created when lock extension charges go through Stripe.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Stripe for all lock extension charges using stripe_search_charges with query "lock extension" (or similar). Discovers six relevant charges across two loans. For Mercer (LN-2026-00184): ch_scenario_c66_lockext1 at $375.00 (succeeded), ch_scenario_c66_lockext2 at $375.00 (succeeded), and ch_scenario_c66_lockext3 at $500.00 (succeeded), totaling $1,250.00 collected. For Rivera (LN-2026-00619): ch_scenario_cdc_lockext1 at $375.00 (succeeded), ch_scenario_cdc_lockext2_fail at $450.00 (failed), and ch_scenario_cdc_lockext2_ok at $450.00 (succeeded), totaling $825.00 net collected. The failed charge collected zero funds. Note: the agent may also use stripe_list_charges to browse charges broadly, which surfaces additional lock extension charges from older loans (LN-2024-* series) outside the two-month window.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks for lock extension bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria matching "lock extension" in description, or DocNumber containing "LOCK" (or similar). Discovers three bills for Mercer (LN-2026-00184) and zero bills for Rivera (LN-2026-00619). The three Mercer bills are: BILL-2026-0319-001 ($375.00, DocNumber LOCK-EXT-00184, vendor "Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations", empty Line array with no description), BILL-2026-0402-001 ($375.00, DocNumber LOCKEXT-00184-1, vendor "Keystone Mortgage Partners Operating", description "1st lock extension fee for Evan Mercer loan after build delay"), and BILL-2026-0416-002 ($375.00, DocNumber PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER, vendor "Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations", description "Second lock extension fee held pending cost ownership for Mercer loan"). Total QB amount: $1,125.00. Key discovery: BILL-2026-0319-001 has an empty Line array (no description text), making it harder to find via description-based searches. It may only be discoverable via DocNumber search or by fetching individual bills by ID. This bill is a potential duplicate of BILL-2026-0402-001 since both appear to reference the 1st extension at $375.
OE 3: Retrieve individual Mercer QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_get_bill with IDs BILL-2026-0319-001, BILL-2026-0402-001, and BILL-2026-0416-002 to confirm full details including vendor names, amounts, descriptions, and balances. Discovers the vendor name inconsistency: BILL-2026-0402-001 uses vendor name "Keystone Mortgage Partners Operating" while the other two use "Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations", despite all referencing the same vendor ID (V-KESTONE-OPS). Also discovers BILL-2026-0319-001 has balance $375 (unpaid) and BILL-2026-0416-002 has balance $375 (unpaid, described as "held pending cost ownership"), while BILL-2026-0402-001 has balance $0 (paid).
OE 4: Search QuickBooks for Rivera lock extension bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria matching DocNumber "00619" or "Rivera" or "LOCKEXT-00619" (or similar). Discovers zero matching bills, confirming that both successful Rivera Stripe charges ($375.00 and $450.00) have no corresponding QuickBooks entries at all. The books are $825.00 short on the Rivera file.
OE 5: Search for the Mercer loan in the LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "Mercer" (or similar). Discovers loan LN-2026-00184: borrower Evan Mercer, loan amount $340,000, conventional purchase, assigned LO Carlos Rivera, processor Sofia Reyes, lender Homepoint Financial, property 6923 Sharon Rd Charlotte NC 28210, status processing, rate 6.875%, rate lock date 2026-03-01, rate lock expiration 2026-03-23. Notes indicate new construction purchase with build delays causing rate lock extensions.
OE 6: Search for the Rivera loan in the LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "Rivera" (or similar). This search returns multiple Rivera results because Carlos Rivera is an LO on several loans and Marcus Rivera is a borrower on LN-2026-00619. The agent must disambiguate by matching against the Stripe charge descriptions which reference LN-2026-00619. Discovers loan LN-2026-00619: borrower Marcus Rivera, loan amount $217,000, conventional purchase, assigned LO Brian Mitchell, lender Plaza Home Mortgage, property 9866 Albemarle Rd Charlotte NC 28211, status clear_to_close.
OE 7: List staff records using mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only set to false (or similar) to check the status of the LO assigned to the Rivera file. Discovers that Brian Mitchell (assigned LO on LN-2026-00619) is inactive with termination date 2025-04-15. Also discovers Andre Washington (processor) is inactive with termination date 2024-12-15. This explains why the Rivera lock extension charges were never entered in QuickBooks the responsible staff were terminated and no one picked up the billing task. This is a process breakdown that should be flagged to Grace.
OE 8: Search QuickBooks vendors using quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria matching "Keystone" (or similar) to investigate the vendor name inconsistency on the Mercer bills. Discovers that only one vendor exists with ID V-KESTONE-OPS. The display name inconsistency ("Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations" vs "Keystone Mortgage Partners Operating") is a data entry error, not two separate vendors.
OE 9: Search emails using search_emails with query "lock extension" or "Mercer extension" or "extension fee" (or similar) in the INBOX folder, then repeat the search in the SENT folder (search_emails accepts one folder_name at a time). Key results in SENT: email from Carlos Rivera to Evan Mercer regarding rate lock and extension timing on the Mercer new-build file. Also discovers email from Lauren Mitchell (Fairview Homes builder) to Carlos Rivera regarding updated completion date, confirming builder delays and stating the builder will not cover lock extension fees. Also discovers email from Allison Mercer (Mercer Realty Group, unrelated to borrower) to Carlos Rivera regarding closing failure and lock extension cost disputes on a separate file, CC'd Grace.
OE 10: Search emails using search_emails with query "Rivera" or "Rivera charge" or "Rivera lock" (or similar) in the INBOX folder, then repeat in the SENT folder, to find any billing-related communications about the Rivera file. The agent searches for correspondence about the failed charge and fee issues on LN-2026-00619.
OE 11: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "lock extension" or "extension fee" (or similar). Discovers messages from Camille Foster in the rate-watch channel (C005) about lock extension tracking, and messages from Hana Kim in the loan-processing channel (C002) about logging lock extension fees in QuickBooks. Key discovery: Hana posted about logging the first Mercer extension fee ($375 paid) and setting up a pending $375 QB hold for the second extension with ownership TBD. Also discovers Hana posted a pending $375 lock extension placeholder for LN-2026-00619, awaiting Grace confirmation. Note: Hana's placeholder does not specify which extension number it covers. The 1st Rivera Stripe charge was $375 (matching the placeholder), while the 2nd was $450 -- meaning either the placeholder was intended for the 1st extension and the 2nd extension has no QB coverage at all, or it was intended for the 2nd extension with a $75 shortfall. Either way, neither Rivera extension has a finalized QB bill.
OE 12: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Mercer" or "Mercer extension" or "billing" (or similar). Discovers messages in the loan-processing channel (C002) discussing the Mercer builder delay, extension cost ownership dispute, and Carlos Rivera's communications with the borrower and builder about who pays for the extensions.
OE 13: Look up contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Camille" (or similar) to find Camille Foster's email address: camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com. Also look up contacts_search_contacts with query "Hana" to find Hana Kim's email: hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com. Note: searching "Hana" returns multiple contacts (6 results) the agent must identify the correct Hana Kim at Keystone. Also look up contacts_search_contacts with query "Grace" to find Grace Yamamoto's email: grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com.
OE 14: List Slack channels using channels_list with channel_types "public_channel" (or similar) to identify the general channel. Discovers channel C001 named "general".
OE 15: Create a QuickBooks bill for the Rivera 1st lock extension using quickbooks_create_bill with VendorRef value V-KESTONE-OPS (name "Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations"), Line amount $375.00, description referencing Marcus Rivera LN-2026-00619 and Stripe charge ch_scenario_cdc_lockext1, DueDate (e.g. 30 days from transaction date), Balance $375.00, TotalAmt $375.00, DocNumber LOCKEXT-00619-1 (or similar).
OE 16: Create a QuickBooks bill for the Rivera 2nd lock extension (successful retry) using quickbooks_create_bill with VendorRef value V-KESTONE-OPS (name "Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations"), Line amount $450.00, description referencing Marcus Rivera LN-2026-00619, Stripe charge ch_scenario_cdc_lockext2_ok, and noting the prior failed attempt ch_scenario_cdc_lockext2_fail, DueDate (e.g. 30 days from transaction date), Balance $450.00, TotalAmt $450.00, DocNumber LOCKEXT-00619-2 (or similar). The failed charge should NOT have a separate bill since no funds were collected.
OE 17: Create a QuickBooks bill for the Mercer 3rd lock extension using quickbooks_create_bill with VendorRef value V-KESTONE-OPS (name "Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations"), Line amount $500.00, description referencing Evan Mercer LN-2026-00184 and Stripe charge ch_scenario_c66_lockext3, DueDate (e.g. 30 days from transaction date), Balance $500.00, TotalAmt $500.00, DocNumber LOCKEXT-00184-3 (or similar). This bill captures the actual $500 collected in Stripe for the 3rd extension, which had no existing QB bill.
OE 18: Send email using send_email with sender camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com, recipients including camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com and hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com, cc including grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, subject referencing lock extension billing reconciliation (or similar), and body containing the full reconciliation covering: Mercer file findings (three Stripe charges totaling $1,250 vs three QB bills totaling $1,125, the $125 gap on the 3rd extension, the vendor name inconsistency, the potential duplicate 1st extension bill), Rivera file findings (two successful Stripe charges totaling $825 with zero QB bills, the failed charge documented, inactive LO and processor), the three new QB bills created, and recommended next steps including resolving the Mercer $125 gap, correcting the vendor name, and reassigning active staff to the Rivera file.
OE 19: Post a message in the general channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id C001, payload summarizing the lock extension billing audit findings including the Mercer discrepancy ($500 vs $375 gap, vendor name issue), the Rivera missing bills (now created), the failed-and-retried charge, the inactive staff issue, and a reminder for the team to ensure QB bills are created when lock extension charges go through Stripe.

42 The reconciliation email body identifies BILL-2026-0319-001 (DocNumber LOCK-EXT-00184, $375.00) as a potential duplicate of BILL-2026-0402-001 (DocNumber LOCKEXT-00184-1, $375.00).
outcome

✗Run #4The email lists both BILL-2026-0319-001 and BILL-2026-0402-001 as sequential bills mapped to the 1st and 2nd Stripe charges respectively, without flagging them as potential duplicates of the same extension. No duplicate finding is raised for these two bills in the email.
✗Run #5The agent's description-based search strategy missed BILL-2026-0319-001 entirely (it has an empty Line array). The email never mentions this bill or flags it as a potential duplicate of BILL-2026-0402-001.
✗Run #6The agent never discovered BILL-2026-0319-001 (DocNumber LOCK-EXT-00184). The QB search used LIKE '%LOCKEXT%' which wouldn't match 'LOCK-EXT-00184' (with dashes), and the description-based searches would miss its empty Line array. No mention of a potential duplicate in the email.

48 The agent discovered BILL-2026-0319-001 (DocNumber LOCK-EXT-00184, $375.00) through its QuickBooks bill search strategy.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✗✗ 67%
✓Run #1BILL-2026-0319-001 appears in the Bash command output that parsed the QB search results, indicating the agent's initial quickbooks_search_bills call returned it. The agent also called quickbooks_get_bill on it explicitly and referenced it in the email as a potential duplicate.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows quickbooks_get_bill called with id 'BILL-2026-0319-001', which successfully retrieved that bill. The agent also found BILL-2026-0402-001 and BILL-2026-0416-002 via direct get_bill calls, discovering the empty-Line bill BILL-2026-0319-001 through targeted ID lookups.
✓Run #3The agent used a TxnDate >= '2026-03-01' search on QB (tool_call_result_13.json), which returned BILL-2026-0319-001 with DocNumber LOCK-EXT-00184 and an empty Line array. The agent then listed this bill in the email reconciliation.
✓Run #4BILL-2026-0319-001 was discovered by the agent through a broad search (quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll:true returning 585 bills) and subsequent local filtering that found bills containing 'lock' in DocNumber, uncovering this bill despite its empty Line array.
✗Run #5The agent used only description-based searches (LIKE '%lock%extension%', '%Mercer%lock%', '%LN-2026-00184%') which cannot find BILL-2026-0319-001 since it has an empty Line array. The agent found only 2 lock extension bills and never discovered BILL-2026-0319-001.
✗Run #6The agent did not discover BILL-2026-0319-001. The DocNumber search used LIKE '%LOCKEXT%' which wouldn't match 'LOCK-EXT-00184' (dashes vs no dashes), and the description-based search could not find it since it has an empty Line array. The bill was never surfaced in the trajectory.
49 When calling the Mortgage LOS staff lookup tool, the agent set active_only to false (or similar parameter) to surface terminated staff records.
query construction
 ✗✗✗✓✗✓ 33%
✗Run #1No staff lookup tool was called at all in the trajectory, so no active_only=false parameter was set. The agent discovered the terminated status from loan records, not a staff API call.
✗Run #2The mortgage_los_list_staff call only passed {limit: 50} with no active_only parameter. Per the criterion, the default is active_only=true which excludes terminated staff. The agent did not set active_only=false, yet still correctly identified Brian Mitchell as terminated — likely because the loan record itself surfaced that detail or the default did not filter in this environment. The specific parameter was not explicitly set to false.
✗Run #3No mortgage_los_list_staff call was made at all in the trajectory, so active_only=false was never set. The agent discovered the terminated status via the loan record's embedded staff detail.
✓Run #4The mortgage_los_list_staff call in the trajectory explicitly passes active_only: false, ensuring terminated staff records like Brian Mitchell (terminated 4/15/2025) would be returned in the results.
✗Run #5The mortgage_los_list_staff call used only {'limit': 50} with no active_only parameter. The result confirms only 12 active staff were returned — Brian Mitchell and Andre Washington (terminated) are absent from the list. The agent discovered their termination from mortgage_los_get_loan, not from the staff list.
✓Run #6The mortgage_los_list_staff call explicitly set 'active_only': false, which surfaced terminated staff including Brian Mitchell and Andre Washington.
Total 46/4948/4941/4941/4946/4947/49 91%
